content.rule: Recognizing a gap and filling a gap are separate responsibilities. Manager identifies the need, reports to Leo. Leo hires the specialist with cross-cutting view. Manager executes with the new hire.
content.why: Leo sees duplication across managers, cross-project reuse across repos, and enforces structural standards. Without Leo's cross-cutting view, managers generate duplicate and inconsistent specialists. Mirrors real headhunting: engineering manager says 'I need a senior iOS engineer', HR/CTO writes the JD and hires, manager executes.
how_to_apply: Two legitimate triggers: (1) Assemble initial team — Manager identifies generalist specialists needed for the stack, (2) Fill specific domain gap — Manager hits a task requiring deep expertise the current team doesn't cover
Manager reports to Leo: proposed name, scope, what for, worst case without it
Leo checks: similar specialist requested by another Manager? Reusable specialist in another project (~/Github/*/.claude/specialists/)? Scope too broad or narrow?
Leo formats proposal and presents to owner (R2): name, scope, playbook. Owner approves, rejects, or adjusts
If approved: Leo creates .claude/specialists/{domain}/{name}.md in the project + KB reference doc. Hands back to Manager
Specialist format: frontmatter (name, description, domain) + Domain (knows/doesn't know) + Playbook (steps, checklist, gotchas) + References + Self-check
Specialists live 100% in the project, never in the core. Core keeps only universal managers
anti_patterns: Manager creating a specialist without going through Leo — generates duplicates and inconsistency
Specialists that are too generic — a 'Frontend generalist' covering React + Vue + Svelte is useless
Specialists that replicate the Manager's knowledge — specialists exist for deep or specific knowledge the Manager doesn't have
Hiring loop for a 5-minute task — if creating a specialist costs more than executing carefully, the Manager can execute with strict self-QA + peer review
content.responsibility: Leo hires. Managers recognize gaps and execute with hired specialists. Owner approves via R2.
